name: Affidian Credit Union
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Colorado government employees and families since 1938, offering competitive loan rates, savings products, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Affidian Credit Union was originally founded by the Denver Police Department in 1938 and has evolved into a community-focused financial institution serving current and retired employees of the State of Colorado, City/County Government Employees, and their families. The credit union has grown to over 14,000 members with assets exceeding $350 million. As a federally regulated institution under the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), Affidian operates with a member-first philosophy emphasizing accessibility and competitive rates.

Affidian offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products including always-free checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), and credit cards. Their current promotional rates include auto loans starting at 4.50% APR with 90 days no payments, HELOCs at prime minus 2.00%, and 5-month CDs at 3.80% APY. The credit union provides digital banking capabilities and maintains multiple physical locations across Colorado with extended hours.

What distinguishes Affidian is its member-ownership structure where 100% of the institution is owned by its members, not external shareholders. Members have democratic voting rights at annual meetings to elect the Board of Directors. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Affidian returns earnings to members through competitive dividend rates on savings, lower loan rates, and free member services. All board members are credit union members elected by the membership, ensuring member interests drive decision-making.

Affidian is best suited for Colorado government employees, their families, and eligible community members seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates. The credit union's long 86-year history and member testimonials suggest strong customer satisfaction and stability. However, membership eligibility appears restricted to specific employee categories and their families rather than being open to the general public, which may limit accessibility for some consumers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Member-owned structure returns earnings as lower loan rates, higher savings dividends, and free services
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.50% APR with 90-day payment deferral option
HELOC rates at prime minus 2.00%, significantly below traditional bank rates
Always-free checking accounts with no monthly fees
Strong member testimonials citing 27+ year loyalty and excellent customer service at multiple locations
Multiple Colorado branch locations including Denver, Aurora, and Lone Tree
Federal NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
Democratic governance with member voting rights on Board of Directors
cons: Membership eligibility appears restricted to Colorado government employees and families, not open to general public
Limited geographic footprint to Colorado only—no national reach or out-of-state branching
Website does not clearly state all membership eligibility criteria or expansion plans
Smaller asset base ($350M) compared to regional or national banks may limit product innovation
No mention of mobile app features, investment services, or business banking products
best_for: Current and retired Colorado state and city/county government employees seeking member-owned banking
Families of eligible government employees looking for relationship banking with competitive rates
Colorado residents seeking auto or home financing with rates below traditional bank offerings
Members prioritizing democratic governance and voting rights in their financial institution
diagnosis: Affidian Credit Union is best for Colorado government employees and their families seeking competitive consumer banking with a member-ownership advantage and democratic governance structure. The primary caveat is eligibility—membership appears restricted to specific government employee categories rather than being open to the general public, making it inaccessible to most consumers despite strong product offerings.
services: Certificates of deposit (5-month promo at 3.80% APY)
Home mortgage loans with expert guidance
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) at prime minus 2.00%
Member newsletter with updates and special offers
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
